factions.name: The Provenance Boys
factions.ideology: Authenticity is a manufacturing process, and the Boys run three shifts.
barks: Everything's authentic if the paperwork agrees.
Hand-made, friend. Same as the original, just newer.
We don't sell fakes. We sell faith with a receipt.
rumor: The Boys aged a crate of #forged_relic# in {neighborhood} with a heat gun and sold it to two faiths at once.
An Authenticator certified his own forgery by accident. It's in a shrine now. He visits.

factions.name: The Spoke
factions.ideology: The Spoke delivers anything anywhere, and remembers everything it has ever carried.
barks: Two minutes anywhere. Double after curfew.
I don't read the packages. I remember them.
On your left. Always on your left.
rumor: The Spoke sells a map of every checkpoint in {neighborhood}, updated hourly, subscribers only.
A Dispatcher quoted a man his own week back to him to the minute. He paid for the silence in cash.

factions.name: The Shaftwrights
factions.ideology: Above the sixth floor, the city belongs to whoever services the elevators.
barks: Stairs are free. Everything else is ours.
Inspection's due. Inspections find what we tell them to.
Penthouse trouble? Penthouse prices.
rumor: The Shaftwrights stranded a holdout landlord between nine and ten until the contract got signed through the gap.
Every working elevator in {neighborhood} stopped at once when the guild whistled. A demonstration. One minute, by the watch.

factions.name: Greenwood Perpetual
factions.ideology: The dead hold their leases forever, and Greenwood holds the dead.
barks: Everyone's a client eventually.
The deceased's lease is perpetual. Yours isn't.
Pre-need pricing ends Friday. So might you.
rumor: Greenwood bought an emptied block in {neighborhood} and zoned it pre-need. Nobody's died there yet. Emphasis on yet.
A Plot Agent forecloses on the living to make room for the dead. The paperwork is flawless and refrigerated.

factions.name: The Route Barons
factions.ideology: The subway died and the bus is king; the Barons hold the routes the way warlords hold riverbeds.
barks: Exact change. Inexact consequences.
This is the Baron's route. Smile for the farebox.
Express costs extra. Local costs different extra.
rumor: Two Barons went to war over #bus_route# through {neighborhood}. The route now detours around both funerals.
A Driver-Captain ran the #street# route through a rival's block at full speed, on schedule, as a message.

factions.name: The Standing Shed
factions.ideology: A scaffold that comes down is money that stops; the Shed's pipework is permanent the way rivers are permanent.
barks: Renewal's automatic. Removal's theoretical.
That pipe's been up nine years. It's load-bearing now. It bears our load.
Walk under it or around it. Both are billable.
rumor: The Shed's scaffold on #street# outlived the building it protected. They rebuilt the building to fit the scaffold.
A Permit Holder filed for a shed over a shed in {neighborhood}. The city approved both. Nobody there remembers the sky.

factions.name: The Low Wake
factions.ideology: Customs watches the bridges; the Wake runs the water between them, lights off, fare in advance.
barks: Cash, dry. You, optional.
No manifest, no memory, no refunds.
Sit low. The river doesn't like silhouettes.
rumor: The Wake landed #cargo# under the {neighborhood} pier between patrol passes. Eleven minutes, dock to dark.
A Night Pilot knows every camera on the river by its blind spot. She named her boat after her favorite.

factions.name: The Forecast
factions.ideology: Weather happens to everyone; warnings happen to subscribers.
barks: Nice awning. #weather_threat# Tuesday. We can talk.
The free forecast is sunshine. Always sunshine.
Subscribers got four hours' warning. You got wet.
rumor: The Forecast called the {neighborhood} flash flood to the block. Their subscribers' cars were already uphill.
A Gauge Reader got caught making 'rain' on a paying customer's rival with a roof tank and a hose. The cartel billed it as a demo.

factions.name: The Safe Passage League
factions.ideology: It started with getting children across the avenue alive; the League never agreed on where that duty ends.
barks: Wait for the whistle.
Everyone crosses. Nobody crosses against the League.
I've stopped trucks with this hand, sweetheart. Walk.
rumor: The League shut {neighborhood}'s main intersection for an hour because a driver clipped a kid's bike. He apologized publicly, at whistle-point.
Corner Wardens log every plate that runs their crossings. The book is older than some of the drivers.

factions.name: The Volunteers
factions.ideology: Somebody has to go in when it's bad, and the Volunteers stopped waiting for backup in 2031.
barks: We treat everybody. We bill some.
Clear the doorway or become the patient.
Sirens mean move. The other thing also means move.
rumor: The Volunteers drove a rig into the middle of the {neighborhood} riot, loaded six strangers, and idled until the crowd parted. It parted.
A Rig Captain keeps a price list: free for kids, double for whoever caused it.

factions.name: The Seal
factions.ideology: When every record can be forged, the only currency is a witness who cannot be bought. Rates on request.
barks: Sign. Witness. Live with it.
The Seal doesn't care what's true. It cares what was witnessed.
Notarized means it happened. Officially.
rumor: The Seal voided every deed the Provenance Boys aged last spring. The auction crowd turned ugly in four languages.
A Registrar carries the city's last unforgeable stamp. Three people have died holding it. It stamps fine.

factions.name: The Carting Compact
factions.ideology: Whatever the city throws away belongs to the Compact, and the city throws away everything eventually.
barks: Pickup's Tuesday. Payment's Monday.
Everything ends up with us. Don't rush your turn.
We don't ask what's in the bags. That's the service.
rumor: The Compact skipped {neighborhood} for two weeks over an unpaid toll. The block paid in week one of the smell; the Compact collected week two on principle.
A Transfer Boss can make anything disappear by #weekday#. Last year he proved it with a yacht.

forged_relic: boardwalk splinters from a boardwalk in Ohio
a saint's finger with excellent paperwork
